fields.generic_name: chlorpheniramine maleate, ibuprofen, and phenylephrine hydrochloride
fields.indication_summary: Temporarily relieves symptoms of hay fever/upper respiratory allergies and the common cold: runny nose, itchy/watery eyes, itching of the nose or throat, sneezing, nasal congestion, sinus pressure, headache, minor body aches and pains, fever.
fields.dosage_forms_strengths: Chlorpheniramine maleate 4 mg; Ibuprofen 200 mg (NSAID); Phenylephrine HCl 10 mg per tablet

notes: OTC 'Drug Facts' format, not the Rx 'Highlights of Prescribing Information' format used by the other 5 documents in this sample set -- no Initial U.S. Approval year listed, and no numbered sections (1 INDICATIONS, 2 DOSAGE, etc), so there is no separate 'Prescribing Information' revision date to compare against the single SPL date this format has. CORRECTED: indication_summary originally dropped two items from the source symptom list ('itching of the nose or throat' and the word 'body' in 'minor body aches and pains') -- re-verified word-for-word against the extracted text and fixed.
